# AGENTS.md — IDU_DVD (DVD IDU)

Guidance for Codex when working in this repository. Part of the **ICII** workspace
(see `../AGENTS.md` for the system-wide picture).

> This guide is stored in **ICII** (`Codex/IDU_DVD.md`), not inside the repo. The repo itself is a git
> submodule at `../IDU_DVD/` (remote `github.com/IDUclub/IDU_DVD`); paths below are relative to that submodule root.

> This repo already has **rich human docs** — read them before deep work:
> `docs/en/architecture.md`, `docs/en/pipeline.md`, `docs/en/api.md`, `docs/en/configuration.md`
> (Russian mirror in `docs/ru/`). This file is the Codex-oriented quick map; the docs are the source of truth.

## What this is

**Document Vector DB**: ingests regulatory documents (`.docx`), reconstructs their logical
structure with an LLM, splits them into meaningful fragments, vectorizes them, and stores them in
**Qdrant** with rich metadata (element type, numbering, hierarchy, version, tags, neighbour links,
cross-document/cross-clause references). On top it serves **vector search** over text and over
tables, with filters (`name`, `document_names`, `version`, `block`, `types`, `tags`) and configurable-width context
assembly, plus a **document listing** endpoint (aggregated by name+version, filterable by
`name`/`version`/`block`/`tags`/upload-time range). Tracks document versions, rejects exact
duplicates, stores tables as separate entities. Exposed via REST **and** an MCP server (`/mcp`).

Reference extraction/linking (`modules/references.py`, `modules/reference_patterns.py`,
`dto/reference.py`) shipped via PR #2 (`feat/doc-links`), squash-merged into `develop` as `4dbb024`
— the branch's own commits aren't ancestors of `develop`, only this single equivalent commit is.

## Tech stack

- Python **3.11–3.13**, managed with **uv** (`uv.lock`). Docker image uses 3.13.
- **FastAPI** + **uvicorn[standard]**.
- **Qdrant** (`qdrant-client`) — vector store.
- **Redis** — parsing-job status + document/version registry.
- **Ollama** — LLM (structure markup, merge, tagging, version detection) + embeddings (`bge-m3`, 1024-d).
- **unstructured[docx]** / python-docx — `.docx` parsing (no OCR backend needed for docx).
- **MinIO** client available (`common/db/minio_client.py`); default upload storage is local disk (`_uploads/`).
- **pydantic-settings** for config, **structlog** for JSON logging.
- Tests: **pytest** + **pytest-asyncio** + **fakeredis**. Versioning: **commitizen**.

## Layout

```
src/
  main.py                       # FastAPI app; mounts MCP at /mcp; documents + search + system routers
  dev_runner.py                 # local entrypoint: python -m src.dev_runner (port 8000, reload)
  __version__.py                # VERSION (kept in sync by commitizen)
  common/
    config/app_config.py        # pydantic-settings Settings, env prefix DVD_  (the config contract)
    db/qdrant_client.py         # Qdrant repository (upsert, search)
    db/redis_client.py          # job status + document/version registry
    db/minio_client.py          # optional object storage client
    logger/, middlewares/       # structlog setup + request logging
  api_clients/ollama_client.py  # LLM + embeddings calls
  dvd_service/
    routers/documents.py        # upload / job status / GET /documents (filtered, aggregated list)
    routers/search.py           # vector search endpoints (search_texts/tables/all filters)
    services/dvd_service.py     # IngestionService, SearchService, DocumentsService
    modules/                    # the pipeline stages (see docs/en/pipeline.md):
      doc_parsers.py            #   Stage 0: parse .docx into blocks
      structure.py              #   Stage 1-2: LLM structure markup + merge
      hierarchy.py              #   build sections/clauses/sub-clauses + neighbour links
      tagging.py                #   fragment tagging
      references.py             #   Stage 5.5: ReferenceExtractor + ReferenceResolver (feat/doc-links)
      reference_patterns.py     #   regex seed + learned-pattern substrate for reference extraction
      windowing.py              #   chunk into windows for structured LLM output
    dto/                         # node_payload, search, upload, document, reference models
  mcp_server/                   # FastMCP server (app.py mounts at /mcp, server.py defines tools)
  system_service/               # /system controller + router (health, logs)
tests/
  unit/                         # hermetic — mock every external boundary (Qdrant/Redis/Ollama)
  integration/                  # need the live local stack; each self-skips if its service is down
```

## Commands

A `Makefile` wraps the common flows (`make help` lists them):

```bash
make install          # uv sync (incl. dev group)
make up               # docker compose up -d qdrant redis  (local infra)
make run              # uv run python -m src.dev_runner  (uvicorn, reload, :8000)
make test             # uv run pytest tests/unit        (fast, hermetic — safe anywhere)
make test-integration # uv run pytest tests/integration -m integration  (needs Qdrant+Redis+Ollama)
make test-all         # full suite
make down             # stop local stack
docker compose up -d --build   # full app + qdrant + redis in containers
uv run pre-commit run --all-files
```

Swagger UI: `http://localhost:8000/docs`. MCP: `http://localhost:8000/mcp`.

## Configuration

All settings live in `src/common/config/app_config.py` (pydantic-settings), overridable via env
vars with the **`DVD_`** prefix or `.env` (see `.env.example` and `docs/en/configuration.md`). Key ones:

| Variable | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `DVD_OLLAMA_BASE`, `DVD_OLLAMA_MODEL`, `DVD_OLLAMA_EMBED_MODEL` | Ollama endpoint + LLM + embedding model |
| `DVD_QDRANT_URL`, `DVD_QDRANT_COLLECTION`, `DVD_VECTOR_SIZE` | Qdrant target; `VECTOR_SIZE` **must** match the embed model (`bge-m3` = 1024) |
| `DVD_REDIS_URL` | Redis (job status + registry) |
| `DVD_ENABLE_REFERENCE_LINKING` | toggles the reference extraction/resolution stage (default on) |
| `DVD_REF_PATTERN_LEARNING` | lets the LLM grow the regex pattern base over time (default off) |
| `DVD_UPLOAD_DIR`, `DVD_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` | Upload path (`./_uploads`), allowed types (`.docx` only) |
| `DVD_LOG_DIR`, `DVD_LOG_FILE`, `DVD_LOG_LEVEL` | JSON logging (also served via `GET /system/logs`) |

In Docker, `docker-compose.yaml` overrides Qdrant/Redis to compose service names and points Ollama
at `host.docker.internal` (Ollama runs on the host, not in compose).

## Conventions

- **Formatting is enforced**: `black` + `isort --profile black`, Python 3.11. Run pre-commit before committing.
- **Conventional Commits** (commitizen). The Release workflow bumps version + changelog from commit
  messages on `master`; `tests.yml` runs format + unit tests on PRs to `develop`. Keep `0.x` (breaking
  → minor bump). Don't hand-edit `src/__version__.py` — commitizen owns it.
- **Tests are tiered**: unit tests must stay hermetic (mock Qdrant/Redis/Ollama); integration tests
  carry the `integration` marker and self-skip when a service is unavailable. Preserve that split.
- Logging is **structlog** JSON; prefer `log.info("event", key=value)` structured calls.

## Gotchas

- `DVD_VECTOR_SIZE` and the embedding model dimension **must agree**, or Qdrant upserts fail.
- Default `ollama_base` in `app_config.py` is an internal host (`http://a.dgx:11434`) — override it
  locally (`.env.example` points at `localhost:11434`).
- The local API and the gMART IDU MCP both default to **port 8000** — don't run them on the same host
  port simultaneously.
- Only `.docx` is supported today (`DVD_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS`); other formats are planned, not implemented.

## Related services

- Knowledge service consumed by the **gMART document-QA agent**: gMART reaches this `/mcp` server via
  its `DVD_MCP_SERVER` env var (the `dvd_mcp_client`) to answer questions over regulatory documents.
  The MCP is **unauthenticated** (no JWT). Distinct from gMART's own IDU MCP (`IDU_MCP_SERVER`).
